page: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-64-lssd is a virtual machine instance from the c4a server family. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 256.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced resource allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU core. The instance does not include any GPU accelerators, and the primary local storage size is designated as None, although the basic configuration description references 14 local SSDs.

This instance does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Performance benchmark data, including CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations, is currently unavailable for this specific instance type. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio position this instance as a standard resource-dense option for workloads requiring consistent, non-shared compute power without the overhead of specialized hardware accelerators.

Due to its balanced 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB RAM profile, the c4a-standard-64-lssd is suited for general-purpose enterprise workloads, multi-threaded application servers, medium-scale web serving, and containerized microservices. The lack of dedicated GPU hardware makes it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated machine learning training, while the dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance for continuous execution tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4a-standard-64-lssd is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine within the c4a server family, configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. This resource distribution yields a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not feature any GPU accelerators, and its primary local storage size is listed as None, despite a basic description referencing 14 local SSDs. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included, and benchmark performance data is currently unavailable. Qualitatively, the dedicated CPU allocation offers stable, predictable performance for general-purpose workloads. This server is best suited for enterprise applications, web serving, and containerized deployments requiring a balanced compute-to-memory ratio without specialized hardware acceleration. It provides a reliable environment for multi-threaded application servers and medium-scale databases.
